title: Rocket Lab (RKLB) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
text: Rocket Lab delivered record-breaking Q1 2026 results, reporting $200.3 million in revenue—a 63.5% year-over-year increase. The company achieved 38.2% GAAP and 43% non-GAAP gross margins while expanding its backlog to $2.2 billion, up 108% annually. Key highlights include booking 31 Electron and HASTE launches (a quarterly record) and securing the largest contract in company history: five Neutron flights plus three Electrons for an undisclosed customer through 2029. The HASTE program now represents nearly one-third of total launch backlog. Management confirmed Rocket Lab's selection alongside Raytheon for the Golden Dome space-based interceptor demonstration program, reinforcing its position in U.S. national security priorities. The company also announced plans to acquire Motive Space Systems and completed its acquisition of Mynaric, establishing a European foothold. Peter Beck noted: We have blown through the ceilings across all of the most important metrics: record revenue, record GAAP gross margins, record backlog, record cash position, and record launch contracts across Electron, HASTE, and Neutron. The company maintains $1.48 billion in cash with over $2 billion in total secured liquidity, positioning it for continued growth and M&A opportunities as Neutron development progresses toward a targeted late 2026 first flight.
